devnotes.summary: Simulates logistic, monomolecular, Gompertz, or Richards growth. It is mainly used for describing or interpolating the crop parameters such as LAI, DM, CropHeight, and ShootN over time, which are needed to simulate soil water and soil nitrogen. The approach depends on the experimental data to which the growth curve is fitted or on temporally dense observations that can be used for interpolation.

remarks.author: Henning Kage & Agronomy Group, University of Kiel

References.item: Kage, H., Kochler, M., Stutzel, H., 2000. Root growth of cauliflower (Brassica oleracea L. botrytis) under unstressed conditions: Measurement and modelling. Plant Soil 223, 131–145.

devnotes.summary: Defines the types of growth curves/interpolation methods available.
Enumeration type for the state variables used in the growth curve plant model
Enumeration type for the parameters used in the growth curve plant model.
devnotes.remarks: Be aware that the meaning of the parameters depends on the growth curve type.

devnotes.summary: Array of state variable names for the growth curve plant model
Array of state variable units for the growth curve plant model
Array of parameter names for the growth curve plant model
Array of parameter units for the growth curve plant model.
devnotes.remarks: Be aware that these units depend on the associated state variables.

devnotes.summary: Maximum number of data points for interpolation

Plant model that simulates growth curves based on logistic, monomolecular, Gompertz, Richards, linear, or expo-linear growth. It calculates growth rates based on temperature and other parameters and integrates these rates over time to update state variables such as LAI, DM, CropHeight, and ShootN.
devnotes.remarks: This class extends TAbstractPlant and provides methods for calculating growth rates, integrating state variables, and handling temperature effects on plant growth. It also supports soil nitrogen uptake if enabled.

devnotes.summary: Indicates if the plant has emerged.
devnotes.remarks: This flag is set to true when the plant has emerged from the soil.

devnotes.summary: Interpolation values for state variables, storing time and value pairs.
Indicates if nitrogen uptake should be simulated.
Calculates the growth rate based on the current state, temperature, and curve-specific parameters.
Getter for the Leaf Area Index (LAI)
devnotes.returns: Leaf Area Index as a THumeNumEntity
devnotes.remarks: This method retrieves the current Leaf Area Index from the state variables.

devnotes.summary: THumeNumEntity is a base class for numerical entities in the model, such as variables and external values.
devnotes.remarks: Extends THumeEntity with properties for numerical values, units, and flags for plotting and output.

devnotes.summary: Getter for the Crop Height
devnotes.remarks: This method retrieves the current crop height from the state variables.

devnotes.summary: THumeNumEntity is a base class for numerical entities in the model, such as variables and external values.
devnotes.remarks: Extends THumeEntity with properties for numerical values, units, and flags for plotting and output.

devnotes.summary: Getter for the Nitrogen uptake rate
devnotes.returns: Nitrogen uptake rate as a THumeNumEntity
devnotes.remarks: This method retrieves the current nitrogen uptake rate from the growth rates.

devnotes.summary: THumeNumEntity is a base class for numerical entities in the model, such as variables and external values.
devnotes.remarks: Extends THumeEntity with properties for numerical values, units, and flags for plotting and output.

devnotes.summary: Indicates if the plant is currently growing
devnotes.remarks: This boolean flag is set to true when the plant is actively growing, false otherwise.

field.name: temp
field.devnotes: Pointer to external temperature variable of type TExternV

devnotes.summary: Temperature sum state variable
Temperature sum state variable for dry matter
Array of state variables for the growth model
Array of growth curve types for each state variable
Array of curve options for each state variable
devnotes.remarks: Options are the user interface to define the type of growth curve used for each state variable.

devnotes.summary: Array of variable changes for each state variable
Two dimensional Array of parameters for each state variable
Temperature sum for emergence
After sowing and before harvest, initialize state variables with their initial values.
Create state variable with name, units, initial value, and optional comment.
Create options for curve type with name, default value, and comment.
Create variable with name, units, initial value, and comment.
